note: 2026-05-31 full window (59 completed runs, 89.8% success). 6 failures across 5 distinct classes. TWO recurrences strengthened: token-budget-429 (#35661) hit a 2nd workflow on a 2nd consecutive day; the safe-output partial-failure-intolerance class hit 2 MORE workflows (Sub-Issue Closer, LintMonster). 1 NEW infra class (threat-detection prompt.txt missing). 2 failures were a transient PR-branch-deleted race (external cause).

issues.title: ESCALATING: one invalid/failed safe-output item red-fails the WHOLE safe_outputs job even when other items succeeded — now 4 distinct workflows
issues.detail: This class (formerly 'safe-output-target-star-no-number') now spans Contribution Check (05-30), Sub-Issue Closer (05-31) and LintMonster (05-31). (A) Sub-Issue Closer 26706621748: 11 add_comment items had target=* with NO item_number on a schedule event, so all 11 failed and the entire safe_outputs job went red DESPITE 11 successful update_issue items in the same batch. (B) LintMonster 26702419759: assign_to_agent for #36048 and #36049 returned GitHub API 'Request failed' errors, and those 2 failed items red-failed the job DESPITE 3 successful create_issue (#36048-#36050) + 1 create_discussion. The common root defect: 'Process Safe Outputs' fails the whole job on ANY failed item rather than skipping-with-warning when >=1 item succeeded. SECONDARY: (1) target=* with no resolvable number on schedule events is structurally unsatisfiable yet emitted repeatedly — validate at the MCP emit boundary so the agent self-corrects in-loop; prompt-only guardrails proved insufficient. (2) assign_to_agent to copilot failed via API — investigate whether the coding-agent assignment endpoint/permission is the cause. FIX (High): make Process Safe Outputs treat individual failed items as skipped-with-warning when >=1 item in the batch succeeded.

issues.title: RECURRING (2nd consecutive day, 2nd workflow): effective-token 25M cap blown -> CAPIError 429 -> all retries re-hit the cap -> run fails
issues.detail: Run 26702042593 (Daily Firewall Logs Collector and Reporter, copilot/sonnet). After 60 turns the copilot harness hit 'CAPIError: 429 Maximum effective tokens exceeded (25411466.70 / 25000000)'. It retried 4 attempts with --continue (isCAPIError400=false, not auth/model), each re-hitting the 429, then 'all 3 retries exhausted — giving up (exitCode=1)' after 18m25s. Identical signature to 05-30's Linter Miner (25.13M/25M). PATTERN: heavy daily-aggregation workflows now reliably exceed the 25M effective-token cap. NOTE: Documentation Noob Tester reached 25.03M eff_tok the same day and SUCCEEDED — it is right at the cliff edge. FIX OPTIONS: (a) chunk/reduce per-run scope of heavy workflows (Daily Firewall Logs, Linter Miner, Documentation Noob Tester); (b) harness FAIL-FAST on a budget-429 (retrying with --continue cannot recover a hard cap — it just burns ~90s x4); (c) raise the budget per-workflow if justified. Maps to open #35661.

issues.title: NEW: threat-detection (detection job) fails when prompt.txt is missing -> detection agent exit 1 -> no THREAT_DETECTION_RESULT -> run red
issues.detail: Run 26703540010 (Code Simplifier, copilot). The AGENT succeeded and produced a valid PR patch+bundle (create_pull_request, has_patch=true), but the DETECTION job failed: the setup step copies /tmp/gh-aw/aw-prompts/prompt.txt -> /tmp/gh-aw/threat-detection/aw-prompts/prompt.txt with 'cp ... 2>/dev/null || true', which silently masked a missing source. The detection agent then hit 'ERR_SYSTEM: Prompt file not found at /tmp/gh-aw/threat-detection/aw-prompts/prompt.txt', exited 1, produced no THREAT_DETECTION_RESULT, and the parse step failed ('ERR_PARSE: No THREAT_DETECTION_RESULT found in detection log'), red-failing the run. Shared detection harness => any create_pull_request workflow whose prompt.txt is absent at detection time is exposed. FIX: do not mask the cp failure; verify the threat-detection prompt exists before invoking the detection agent and fail with a clear, actionable error (or regenerate it), rather than letting the agent hard-fail on a missing prompt.

issues.title: TRANSIENT (external cause): PR-triggered workflows fail when the PR head branch is deleted/force-pushed before checkout
issues.detail: Two runs fired on PR head branch 'copilot/update-agentic-workflows-and-skill-file' which no longer existed on the remote. (A) Test Quality Sentinel 26700870332: 'fatal: couldn't find remote ref copilot/update-agentic-workflows-and-skill-file' -> git fetch exit 22, agent never ran (0 turns). (B) Design Decision Gate 26700870336 (claude): agent ran 21 turns but push_to_pull_request_branch/incremental-patch generation failed ('Branch HEAD does not exist locally' / 'couldn't find remote ref'). Root cause is external — the Copilot PR branch was merged/deleted while these PR-event workflows were queued/running. Low priority; consider a graceful 'PR branch no longer exists -> skip cleanly (neutral)' guard at checkout so a deleted branch yields a skip rather than a red failure.

issues.title: WATCH (new): Go Logger Enhancement $8.30 / 41.9M eff_tok / 117 turns — today's most expensive run (above the prior $7.15 peak), but SUCCEEDED
issues.detail: Run 26703472897 (claude/opus). 12.38M raw / 41.91M effective tokens over 117 turns, $8.30 — the single most expensive run of the day and a new daily-cost high (prior peak was 05-30's Failure Investigator $7.15, which had FAILED). This one succeeded, so it is value-for-cost rather than waste, but 117 turns / 41.9M eff_tok is a heavy-tail outlier worth watching for repeat or growth.
